title: Deployments, ReplicaSets, Rollouts, Strategies, Conditions, and Recovery · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in deployments, replicasets, rollouts, strategies, conditions, and recovery, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Trace Deployment template hash, ReplicaSet ownership, desired replicas, Pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status.
Calculate RollingUpdate maxSurge, maxUnavailable, total capacity, readiness, termination, and rollout duration under a changed replica count.
Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 2 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. Bind immutable identity, strategy, conditions, pause, rollback, and verification. steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Rollback Causal Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery-1y2kvu". Tailor its deployment.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Rollback Causal Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Rollback Causal Recovery into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Rollback Causal Recovery, keep K8s Rollback Causal Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery-1y2kvu
  - competency: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery
    artifact: deployment.yaml
    decision: rollback causal recovery
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: change a selector so one canary endpoint overlaps production
    retained-proof: authorization review token audience and audit correlation
    case-key: 1y2kvu
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
    repair: Bind image and config identity, calculate surge and unavailable capacity, inspect conditions and ReplicaSets, and coordinate roll forward or rollback.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
